name: Alcornocal de Tolibes Trail
name_original: Alcornocales de Tolibes – circular desde Potes
description: Hike the Cork oaks of Tolibes circular route from Potes, a moderate 4.1-mile (6.6 km) trail through forests and riverbanks.
region_intro: Hiking around Potes offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Picos de Europa National Park in Cantabria, Spain. The region is characterized by jagged limestone mountains, deep gorges, and lush river valleys formed by the Deva and Quiviesa rivers. Hikers can explore diverse terrains, from shaded forest paths through cork oak and sweet chestnut trees to high-altitude mountain trails and expansive pastures. Best hiking trails around Potes The most popular hiking route is Alcornocal de Tolibes Trail, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) moderate trail that takes about 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, leading through forests and along riverbanks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lebaniego Way, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This route offers views of the Picos de Europa and leads to the Monasterio de Santo Toribio de Liébana. Local hikers also love the Trail to the Cross of La Viorna from Potes, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Potes is defined by dramatic mountain ranges, deep river gorges, and extensive forests. The network of trails offers options for easy strolls, moderate forest walks, and challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Potes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 350 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Potes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Potes?
A: Potes offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. You'll find routes through shaded forests, along river valleys, and up to high-altitude mountain pastures within the Picos de Europa National Park. There are 31 routes available, with 8 easy, 19 moderate, and 4 difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes near Potes?
A: Yes, Potes has several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206575654/lebaniego-way" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebaniego Way&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.0-mile (6.4 km) path that offers views of the Picos de Europa and leads to the historic Monasterio de Santo Toribio de Liébana. The Riega de Maredes valley also features shaded paths with small waterfalls, perfect for a gentle stroll.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on trails around Potes?
A: Many trails in the Picos de Europa National Park and surrounding areas are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes from Potes?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001147484/alcornocal-de-tolibes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcornocal de Tolibes Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.1-mile (6.6 km) circular route that takes you through forests and along riverbanks. For a more challenging option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Potes – La Viorna Cross loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking near Potes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4767542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcornocal de Tolibes&lt;/a&gt;, Cantabria's largest cork oak forest, or hike to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3601813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viorna Cross&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the Picos de Europa. The Riega de Maredes valley also features a stream with several small waterfalls.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Potes?
A: The best time for hiking in Potes is generally from spring to autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is milder, and trails are more accessible. Summer offers warm, sunny days, while spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Higher elevations may still have snow in early spring or late autumn.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in the Potes area besides hiking?
A: Beyond hiking, Potes is a hub for various outdoor adventures. You can try via ferrata, canyoning, spelunking, rafting, or kayaking on the Deva and Cares rivers. Horseback riding, quad biking, and e-mountain biking are also popular. In winter, higher elevations offer opportunities for backcountry skiing or snowshoeing.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer spectacular views of the Picos de Europa?
A: Absolutely. Many trails provide stunning vistas of the Picos de Europa. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001177713/trail-to-the-cross-of-la-viorna-from-potes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Cross of La Viorna from Potes&lt;/a&gt; leads to the summit of Cruz de la Viorna (1151 meters), offering extensive views of the eastern Picos massif and the Liébana valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jano Peak&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent destination for panoramic views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Potes?
A: The trails around Potes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic mountain ranges, deep river gorges, and extensive forests, highlighting the variety of terrain and the stunning views of the Picos de Europa.

Q: How can I access high-altitude trails in the Picos de Europa from Potes?
A: A popular way to access high-altitude trails is via the Fuente Dé Cable Car, located a short drive from Potes. This cable car quickly transports visitors 800 meters higher to an altitude of 1823 meters, serving as an excellent starting point for beautiful high-mountain hikes and trekking adventures.

Q: Are there any visitor centers or information points for the Picos de Europa National Park near Potes?
A: Yes, the Sotama Visitor Center in Tama, located near Potes, serves as a Picos de Europa National Park visitor center. It offers educational exhibits about the park's ecology and geology, as well as trail maps and information to help you plan your hikes.
tips: It is located on top of a small hill at the foot of Pico Pumar. The hermitage dates from the year 990, every year on August 6 there is a big party and the holy image is carried around.

A forest of cork oaks that can be covered almost entirely by following the large number of paths that there are through it and having at your hands these curious trees from which the cork is extracted. A very quiet and picturesque place with beautiful views of the surrounding environment.

Potes is one of the most beautiful towns in Cantabria and Spain. It has a privileged position to visit the Picos de Europa. It is near the point of arrival of the Camino Lebaniego and frankly, it is worth staying there at night to rest and take advantage of its restaurants and its small streets.

faqs.question: How long does the Cork oaks of Tolibes circular route take?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically takes around 2 hours and 13 minutes to complete. This is an estimated duration for hiking the approximately 6.57 km trail.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This is considered a moderate hike. While it primarily follows paths and tracks, there are some potentially steep sections, especially at the beginning, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. The route involves an ascent and descent of about 330-340 meters.

faqs.question: Where does the Cork oaks of Tolibes trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts from the charming medieval town of Potes, specifically near the San Cayetano bridge. Potes offers various parking options, and it's recommended to find parking within the town before beginning your hike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Cork oaks of Tolibes trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a rich experience. You'll pass through the largest cork oak forest in Cantabria, known as the Alcornocal de Tolibes. Other highlights include the ruins of an old medieval monastery dedicated to Santiago, the abandoned village of Porcieda, and the Hermitage of the Virgin of Valmayor. The starting point, the Historic center of Potes, is also a significant attraction.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, consisting mostly of paths and tracks. You'll also encounter sections on paved roads, unpaved tracks, and even a small part on a national road. Be prepared for some steep ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Liébana region, where this trail is located, experiences a Mediterranean climate conducive to the cork oaks. While specific recommendations aren't given, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery for hiking in this area, avoiding the heat of summer or potential winter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Cork oaks of Tolibes trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there are no explicit restrictions against dogs. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas like Potes or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pets in natural environments.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike the Cork oaks of Tolibes trail. While the area is part of the Liébana Site of Community Importance, it is generally open for public access without special permits for hiking.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the path offers beautiful views of the surrounding environment. From higher points, you can enjoy impressive vistas of the Picos de Europa and the peaks of Peña Sagra. The ruins of the Santiago Medieval Monastery also provide wonderful panoramic views of the area.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find parts of the Camino Vadiniense, the Camino Lebaniego, and the Senda de la Reserva del Saja. It also follows sections of local paths like Calle de la Solana, Paseo del Río Deva, Calle de la Fuente de la Riega, and Calle de Santa Olaja.

faqs.question: What unique historical features can I discover along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty, the trail offers a glimpse into history. You can explore the abandoned village of Porcieda, which provides insight into traditional regional architecture. The route also follows a segment of the historic Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route, and you can see the ruins of an old medieval monastery dedicated to Santiago.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Valmayor
